danger_signals.visual_form: Foot Clan soldiers, Bebop, Rocksteady, Fire Freak sprites in red/dark colors
danger_signals.meaning: Combat threat; areas controlled by villain faction

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger, distinct sprite designs (Shredder, Technodrome); flinching animation states
danger_signals.meaning: High-threat encounter; progression gate; major antagonist presence

danger_signals.visual_form: Large mechanical fortress graphic (mini variant in design; full version as level environment)
danger_signals.meaning: Villain headquarters; stronghold of enemy faction; final danger zone
World under threat; faction dominance; rescue objective signals danger ahead

safety_signals.visual_form: Character selection screen showing four distinct turtle sprites (each with unique color identity)
safety_signals.meaning: Multiple lives/resources available; player agency; safe hub state

safety_signals.visual_form: Overhead exploration areas with benign environmental tiles
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones; friendly territory; home base before combat stages

safety_signals.visual_form: April NPC sprite (supporting character in narrative)
safety_signals.meaning: Ally; friendly faction indicator; narrative anchor for safety/mission briefing

faction_indicators.color_identity: Four distinct shell colors: Leonardo (blue), Raphael (red), Donatello (purple), Michelangelo (orange)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Ninja-themed weapons (katanas, sai, bo staff, nunchaku); turtle shell silhouettes
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Sewers, New York urban environments, turtle van (unused open state frame exists)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Shredder armor/claw weapon; generic ninja foot soldiers; Foot Clan emblem (implicit in enemy spawn)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Bebop and Rocksteady (mutant henchmen with unused flinching frames); Fire Freak (specialized variant with unused flinching); standard soldiers
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Technodrome fortress (villain stronghold); industrial/tech environments; dark urban areas under faction control

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Wise sage; father figure (especially in Japanese localization where April is reframed as Splinter's daughter)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Initially in captive state; requires rescue; appears in end-sequence narrative (heart symbol appears in Japanese ending text)

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Enemy faction dominance / Foot Clan control
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dark tile palettes in controlled areas; increased enemy spawn density; Technodrome visible as environmental backdrop or level zone
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol aggressively; captive allies (Splinter) remain imprisoned; faction forces block progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Overhead exploration view; lighter color palette; minimal enemy presence; April and friendly NPCs visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Free movement; mission briefing opportunities; character switching between turtles (turtle can be swapped as 'glorified extra life' mechanic)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Larger sprite graphics; Shredder or henchman (Bebop, Rocksteady, Fire Freak) appear; Technodrome interior as stage setting
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss enters flinching state when hit; unique attack patterns; high-difficulty combat encounter

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cutscene with door open sound effect (JP version) and text dialogue; heart symbol (JP only, modified in international releases); April and Splinter sprite states change
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dialogue sequence; resolution of captive storyline; mission completion state

environmental_storytelling.example: Splinter appears imprisoned during level progression; rescue objective implicitly communicates faction threat and player mission

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Large fortress structure visible or accessible as final level; mini-Technodrome graphics suggest scale and threat
environmental_storytelling.example: Unused mini-Technodrome graphic (grouped with Area 3 boss graphics) indicates enemy stronghold significance; final level takes place inside/at this structure

environmental_storytelling.technique: Overhead exploration to side-scroll combat transition
environmental_storytelling.example: Game structure shifts between safe overhead areas (mission prep) and dangerous side-scrolling combat zones (enemy territory); spatial layout communicates progression into danger
Character selection screen with four distinct shell colors means player visually learns turtle identity; logo color doesn't change per turtle in JP version, but sprite color always distinct

Japanese localization reframes April as Splinter's daughter (otōsan/'father' address), changing family/alliance storytelling; European censorship renames game 'Teenage Mutant Hero Turtles' (no ninja reference), altering faction identity perception

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Flinches with dedicated animation frames; shows vulnerability; signals successful player action
Unused flinching frames exist but go unused in-game (never flinch from player attacks); suggests simplified AI or design constraint; visual preparedness unused
Appears imprisoned in level; triggers end-sequence dialogue when rescued; presence signals world-state threat
Spawn in controlled territory; attack on contact; represent faction presence throughout levels
Character switches to next available turtle (treated as extra life); visual communication of resource depletion; swappable state allows continued play

notes: Game communicates world-state primarily through color-coded turtle identities, environmental transitions (overhead safe zones vs. side-scroll combat danger), and enemy/boss presence. Faction identity (Foot Clan vs. Turtles/Splinter) expressed through sprite design and territorial control of level geography. Regional differences (Japanese title 'Geki Kame Ninja Den', European censorship of 'Ninja' term, April-as-daughter reframe) alter political/narrative messaging without changing core visual manifestation. Unused assets (flinching frames for bosses, mini-Technodrome, open turtle van, 'THANKS' speech bubble, heart symbol) suggest design iteration where additional state-communication was planned but cut. Sound cues (door opening in JP cutscenes) provide secondary world-state signals. Tier T2 assessment suitable: qualitative visual patterns documented but no numerical hitpoint/damage/behavior metrics available in sources.
